epic.title: Infrastructure State Reconciliation System
epic.product_focus: Infrastructure as Code / Terraform / Cloud Architecture

submission.reviewer_notes: Run in DEMO_MODE=true for a zero-credential demo. Set DEMO_MODE=false with optional AWS and LLM env vars for live mode. Both modes expose the full feature set. Start backend: `cd backend && npm run dev`. Start frontend: `cd frontend && npm run dev`. Open http://localhost:5174.

repo_access.access_notes: Repository is on the local Labs/AI/artifact-drift path. Branch: main. All work is committed.

notable_outputs: Three-layer infrastructure graph (Planned Architecture / Terraform State / AWS Runtime) rendered in ReactFlow
Drift detection engine: 8 drift types, index-based fuzzy matching, attribute/tag/SG comparison
Live AWS inventory via SDK with automatic mock fallback (examples/aws-mock-inventory.json)
LLM-powered static diagram interpreter: Claude vision API extracts resources and relationships from uploaded PNG/JPG architecture diagrams
Structured drift findings with per-type Terraform remediation commands and compliance score
Workspace-scoped Drift page: cascade Project → Workspace → Scan Run dropdowns, findings scoped to selected run
SECURITY.md — threat model, whitelist specification, redaction controls

safety.sensitive_data_concerns: The backend/.env and backend/data/artifact-drift.db files are in .gitignore and are not committed. All examples use synthetic mock data. No real AWS account IDs, ARNs, or credentials appear in the repository.

known_gaps: No automated test suite — no .test.ts or .spec.ts files present; manual validation via scan runs
No demo notebook — walkthrough is done live via the web UI
Drift detection uses single-region, single-account AWS inventory; multi-account/multi-region is future work
detectDrift() in analysis.ts is now dead code (superseded by DriftAnalysisAgent engine) but not yet removed
